top_signals.title: FirstFT: Oil falls below $80 as traders bet Strait of Hormuz flows will return
top_signals.summary: Also in today’s newsletter: Bank of Japan raises rates to 1%, and India temporarily blocks Telegram
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Taiwan’s Energy Crisis Shows the Cost of Import Dependence
top_signals.summary: The issue of energy dependence and its consequences has, in recent months, become a major topic of discussion. The Hormuz crisis has brought energy security to the political agenda with a vengeance amid fuel price caps, rationing, and warnings of severe shortages.  A lot of countries have been used as examples of either low dependence and therefore insulation against shocks, or high dependence that has resulted in a lot of economic pain. In the latter category, Taiwan stands out as a particularly noteworthy case: the nation depends on imports…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Switzerland loses its shine as central bank gold vault
top_signals.summary: After banks were net sellers in March, they added a net 17 tonnes of gold in April, led mainly by Poland and China.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Poland and World Bank agree end to development loans
top_signals.summary: Deal to graduate from main borrowing programme marks milestone in country’s economic transformation
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
